refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, visibility, and adaptive control. This supports both Site Manager accountability and Operator/Tele-operator decision-making.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = (total_energy_consumed_kWh / total_tonnage_moved) over the evaluation period. Verify that measured value ≤ E_target. Collect telemetry from Vehicle subsystem (power draw, load, distance) and correlate with task completion logs from Tasking interface.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) and work output (tonnage moved) at the Vehicle level.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback (energy_per_ton KPI) to operators and tele-operators via the Telemetry interface.

CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall support operational control strategies (e.g., route optimization, load balancing, idle reduction) that minimize energy_per_ton without compromising safety or task completion.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall log and report energy efficiency metrics to Site Manager for acceptance verification against E_target over the evaluation period.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Passive energy monitoring only (measure and log, no active control or feedback)
DP-B: Active energy management system with real-time feedback loop (measure → display → operator adjusts behavior → measure impact)
DP-C: Autonomous energy optimization with predictive control (system automatically adjusts vehicle parameters, route, load distribution to minimize energy_per_ton)
DP-D: Hybrid approach: real-time feedback + operator-guided optimization + safety-constrained autonomous micro-adjustments
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid approach—integrate real-time energy efficiency feedback (Telemetry) to operators/tele-operators, enable operator-guided control strategies (via Tasking), and implement safety-constrained autonomous micro-adjustments (idle reduction, load balancing) within the Vehicle subsystem. This balances operator agency, safety criticality, and efficiency gains.

potential_sources: Energy measurement (Vehicle) must couple with feedback display (Telemetry interface) and operator control (Tasking interface).
Safety constraints (SafetyAlert) must couple with autonomous optimization to prevent unsafe efficiency-seeking behavior (e.g., excessive speed, overload).
Network latency (Network Link) may degrade real-time feedback quality for tele-operators; mitigation required.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is decoupled from specific vehicle architecture (engine type, drivetrain) and from specific optimization algorithms, allowing flexibility. Coupling is primarily at interface level (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert), which is manageable.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle subsystem can reliably measure energy consumption and work output (tonnage, distance) with acceptable accuracy.
Assumption: Operators and tele-operators will respond to real-time feedback and adjust behavior to improve efficiency.
Risk: Autonomous micro-adjustments may conflict with operator intent or safety constraints if not properly bounded.
Risk: Network latency for tele-operators may delay feedback, reducing effectiveness of real-time loop.
Risk: Defining 'safety-constrained' optimization boundaries requires close coordination with Safety Services.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the boundary between operator-guided control (Tasking) and autonomous micro-adjustments. Define explicit safety constraints (e.g., maximum speed reduction, load limits) that autonomous optimization must respect. Specify acceptable latency for Telemetry feedback to tele-operators.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the end-to-end energy efficiency capability, including: (1) integration of energy measurement from Vehicle, (2) real-time feedback generation and delivery via Telemetry, (3) operator control interface via Tasking, (4) safety constraint enforcement via SafetyAlert, and (5) reporting and logging for Site Manager acceptance verification.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs real-time energy efficiency KPI (energy_per_ton, instantaneous power draw, cumulative tonnage) to operators, tele-operators, and Site Manager dashboards.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task assignments (routes, load targets) and sends back task status and energy efficiency impact estimates. Operators/tele-operators may adjust task parameters to optimize efficiency.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services, which bound the autonomous optimization logic (e.g., do not reduce speed below safe threshold, do not exceed load limits).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution communicates with Vehicle subsystem and Teleoperation module via Network Link to exchange telemetry, tasking, and safety data.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integrator and owns the energy efficiency KPI capability. All sub-functions (measurement, feedback, control, reporting) are logically contained within the solution boundary. Minimal diffusion: only Network Link interface crosses to Vehicle and Teleoperation.

Vehicle subsystem owns energy measurement hardware and autonomous micro-adjustments (idle reduction, load balancing). However, energy efficiency KPI computation, feedback generation, and operator control logic belong to NGHE Solution. High diffusion risk if energy optimization logic is split between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

Teleoperation module handles remote operator interface and control. Energy efficiency feedback display and tele-operator control inputs are part of Teleoperation, but the energy efficiency logic and KPI computation belong to NGHE Solution. Significant diffusion if energy optimization is embedded in Teleoperation.

Safety Services owns safety constraint definition and alert generation. Energy efficiency optimization must respect these constraints, but the optimization logic itself is not a Safety Services responsibility. High diffusion if safety-constrained optimization is embedded in Safety Services.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency capability is best contained within NGHE Solution as the system-level orchestrator. Vehicle subsystem provides measurement and autonomous micro-adjustments; Teleoperation provides operator interface; Safety Services provides constraint boundaries. NGHE Solution integrates these via Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces.
containment_risks: Risk: If energy measurement logic is not standardized across Vehicle variants, NGHE Solution may struggle to compute consistent energy_per_ton KPI.
Risk: If autonomous micro-adjustments in Vehicle are not coordinated with NGHE Solution's optimization logic, conflicting control actions may occur.
Risk: If Teleoperation displays energy feedback without context (e.g., target E_target, progress toward goal), tele-operators may not understand how to adjust behavior.
Risk: If Safety Services constraints are not clearly communicated to NGHE Solution, optimization may violate safety boundaries.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish a clear data contract between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for energy measurement (units, sampling rate, accuracy). Define the scope of autonomous micro-adjustments in Vehicle (e.g., idle reduction only, no speed/load changes). Specify the Telemetry payload to include context (E_target, evaluation period progress, safety constraints). Create a SafetyAlert schema that explicitly bounds optimization parameters.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (hybrid real-time feedback + operator-guided + safety-constrained autonomous optimization) directly addresses the refined FR. The DP enables operators and tele-operators to achieve energy_per_ton ≤ E_target through integrated monitoring, feedback, and control. The DP is not over-constrained and allows flexibility in implementation. Coupling is manageable at the interface level.

diffusion_risks: Energy measurement logic must be owned by Vehicle; NGHE Solution must not duplicate measurement code.
Autonomous micro-adjustments must be owned by Vehicle; NGHE Solution must not override or conflict with Vehicle-level control.
Real-time feedback generation and KPI computation must be owned by NGHE Solution; Vehicle must not compute KPI independently.
Operator control interface (Tasking) must be owned by NGHE Solution; Teleoperation must not bypass NGHE Solution to control Vehicle directly.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution as the IM boundary is appropriate. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are external modules that provide inputs/constraints. The key is to establish clear interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) to prevent diffusion and ensure NGHE Solution owns the energy efficiency orchestration logic.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via field measurement: collect energy_per_ton data over the evaluation period and compare against E_target. Telemetry interface provides real-time data; logging and reporting via NGHE Solution enable post-evaluation analysis. Site Manager can verify acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the energy efficiency KPI capability and integrates inputs from Vehicle (measurement), Teleoperation (operator control), and Safety Services (constraints). Each module has a defined role and interface.

recommended_revisions: Establish a formal data contract between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for energy measurement (units, sampling rate, accuracy, latency).
Define the scope and limits of autonomous micro-adjustments in Vehicle (e.g., idle reduction, load balancing) and how they interact with NGHE Solution's optimization logic.
Specify the Telemetry payload schema to include energy_per_ton KPI, instantaneous power, cumulative tonnage, E_target, evaluation period progress, and safety constraint status.
Create a SafetyAlert schema that explicitly bounds optimization parameters (e.g., minimum safe speed, maximum load, no-go zones).
Define acceptable latency for Telemetry feedback to tele-operators (e.g., < 500 ms for real-time responsiveness).
Establish a feedback loop mechanism: operators adjust behavior based on Telemetry → NGHE Solution logs impact → Site Manager reviews progress toward E_target.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 is not atomic; it requires decomposition into four sub-FRs (measurement, feedback, control, reporting). Each sub-FR has distinct DP and IM implications. Proceeding to the next level will clarify ownership boundaries, interface contracts, and verification strategies for each sub-capability. The refined FR and selected DP are sound; the next level will detail the implementation architecture.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) and work output (tonnage moved) at the Vehicle level.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback (energy_per_ton KPI) to operators and tele-operators via the Telemetry interface.

CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall support operational control strategies (e.g., route optimization, load balancing, idle reduction) that minimize energy_per_ton without compromising safety or task completion.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall log and report energy efficiency metrics to Site Manager for acceptance verification against E_target over the evaluation period.

assumptions: Vehicle subsystem can reliably measure energy consumption and work output with acceptable accuracy.
Operators and tele-operators will respond to real-time feedback and adjust behavior to improve efficiency.
Safety constraints can be clearly defined and communicated to NGHE Solution's optimization logic.
Network Link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency for real-time Telemetry feedback to tele-operators.
E_target is defined by the customer and is achievable with the selected DP (hybrid feedback + operator-guided + autonomous optimization).
open_risks: Autonomous micro-adjustments in Vehicle may conflict with operator intent or safety constraints if boundaries are not clearly defined.
Network latency for tele-operators may degrade real-time feedback effectiveness.
Energy measurement accuracy across Vehicle variants may vary, affecting KPI consistency.
Operator behavior change (response to feedback) is uncertain; effectiveness depends on training and incentives.
Safety constraint boundaries for optimization must be validated with Safety Manager to prevent unsafe efficiency-seeking behavior.
evidence_needed: Vehicle subsystem specification: energy measurement capability, accuracy, sampling rate, latency.
Teleoperation subsystem specification: feedback display capability, operator interface responsiveness.
Safety Services specification: safety constraint definitions, SafetyAlert schema, constraint enforcement mechanisms.
Network Link specification: bandwidth, latency, reliability for Telemetry and Tasking interfaces.
Customer definition of E_target and evaluation period.
Operator training plan and incentive structure for efficiency improvement.
Field trial data or simulation results showing feasibility of achieving E_target with DP-D.
